title: Reddit stock drops 9% as Google AI content deal nears expiration
excerpt: Reddit's $60 million-a-year deal with Google is ending soon, and the company is reassessing whether renewing it makes sense
content_html:
Reddit stock dropped 9% on Wednesday after the Wall Street Journal reported the company had internally discussed ending Google's ability to access its content for AI training purposes.

Reddit and Google struck a $60 million-a-year deal in 2024 allowing Google to train its AI models on Reddit's content, according to CNBC. That agreement is ending soon, and the two companies are in talks about potentially renewing it. With Google's AI-powered query responses cutting into traffic to third-party sites, Reddit's leadership is questioning whether the arrangement still serves the company's interests, the Journal reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

Reddit is not alone. USA Today, Politico, the Economist, People Inc. and Reuters are all weighing how, or even if, they will continue working with Google. USA Today's flagship national outlet saw its organic Google search traffic from U.S. readers decline by close to 50% in the twelve months ending June 2026, per Semrush data. Over that same twelve-month stretch, Politico saw its Google search traffic decline 23%, CNN's fell roughly 25%, and Business Insider experienced a loss of more than 85%.

"This is existential for some categories of publishers," David Buttle, CEO of media consulting firm DJB Strategies, said.

In response, Google said its AI search tools direct substantial traffic across the web and, in a statement to the Journal, said those features support audience growth for publishers and content creators.

Reddit also has a data licensing partnership with OpenAI that allows the ChatGPT maker to train its models on Reddit's content. Reddit CEO Steve Huffman addressed both relationships on a recent earnings call. "We have important partnerships with both Google and OpenAI," Huffman said. "Those are very meaningful to us, and I think it's mutual. We continue to value those."

Reddit reported first-quarter revenue of $663 million, a 69% jump from a year earlier, with advertising driving the bulk of that growth. Data licensing, which includes the Google and OpenAI partnerships, generated $39 million in the quarter. Reddit is scheduled to report second-quarter earnings on July 30.

Reddit's share price has fallen approximately 27% since the start of the year.

content_html_zh:
《华尔街日报》报道称，在 Reddit 内部讨论终止谷歌出于人工智能培训目的访问其内容的能力后，Reddit 股价周三下跌 9%。

据 CNBC 报道，Reddit 和谷歌于 2024 年达成了每年 6000 万美元的协议，允许谷歌根据 Reddit 的内容训练其人工智能模型。 该协议即将到期，两家公司正在就续签的可能性进行谈判。 据《华尔街日报》援引知情人士的话报道，随着谷歌人工智能驱动的查询响应减少了第三方网站的流量，Reddit 的领导层正在质疑这种安排是否仍然符合公司的利益。

Reddit 并不孤单。 《今日美国》、《政治》、《经济学人》、《人物》和《路透社》都在考虑如何或是否继续与谷歌合作。 根据 Semrush 的数据，截至 2026 年 6 月的 12 个月里，《今日美国》这家全国旗舰媒体的美国读者自然 Google 搜索流量下降了近 50%。 在同一 12 个月内，Politico 的 Google 搜索流量下降了 23%，CNN 的搜索流量下降了约 25%，Business Insider 的搜索流量下降了 85% 以上。

媒体咨询公司 DJB Strategies 的首席执行官 David Buttle 表示：“这对于某些类别的出版商来说是生死攸关的。”

作为回应，谷歌表示，其人工智能搜索工具在网络上引导了大量流量，并在给《华尔街日报》的一份声明中表示，这些功能支持出版商和内容创作者的受众增长。

Reddit 还与 OpenAI 建立了数据许可合作伙伴关系，允许 ChatGPT 制造商根据 Reddit 的内容训练其模型。 Reddit 首席执行官史蒂夫·霍夫曼 (Steve Huffman) 在最近的财报电话会议上谈到了这两种关系。 “我们与谷歌和 OpenAI 都有重要的合作伙伴关系，”霍夫曼说。 “这些对我们来说非常有意义，我认为这是相互的。 我们继续重视这些。”

Reddit 公布第一季度收入为 6.63 亿美元，同比增长 69%，其中广告推动了大部分增长。 数据许可（包括 Google 和 OpenAI 合作伙伴）在本季度创造了 3900 万美元的收入。 Reddit 定于 7 月 30 日公布第二季度收益。

自今年年初以来，Reddit 的股价已下跌约 27%。
